meta.title: Charaka Samhita Knowledge Module
meta.description: Core Ayurvedic principles from Charaka Samhita for daily health coaching

topics.title: The 8 Factors of Proper Eating
topics.description: Charaka identifies 8 factors for ideal food: Prakriti (nature), Karana (processing), Samyoga (combination), Rashi (quantity), Desha (place of origin), Kala (time/season), Upayokta Sanstha (rules of eating), Upayokta (the individual).
topics.daily_tip: Eat freshly cooked warm food in a calm environment. Avoid eating standing up or while distracted.

dosha_impact.kapha: light dry spiced food pacifies kapha

topics.title: Brahma Muhurta - Rise Before Sunrise
topics.shloka: Brahme muhurte uttishthet swastho rakshartham ayushah
topics.description: Wake up during Brahma Muhurta (1.5 hours before sunrise) for optimal health. This time is rich in prana, sattva predominates, and the mind is clear for meditation and planning.
topics.daily_tip: Set alarm for 5:00-5:30 AM. Begin with gratitude, water sipping, and 10 min meditation before checking phone.

dosha_impact.vata: reduces anxiety when routine is consistent

topics.title: Jatharagni - The Digestive Fire
topics.description: Jatharagni (digestive fire) is the root of life. Strong agni = long healthy life. Weak agni = disease. All diseases begin from impaired digestion (mandagni).
topics.daily_tip: Sip warm water throughout the day. Eat main meal at noon when agni is strongest. Avoid eating when not hungry.

dosha_impact.vata: irregular agni - eat at fixed times
dosha_impact.kapha: slow agni - use digestive spices like ginger

topics.title: Trigunas in Food - Sattvic, Rajasic, Tamasic
topics.description: Sattvic foods (fresh, juicy, nourishing) bring clarity and peace. Rajasic foods (spicy, hot, salty) bring restlessness. Tamasic foods (stale, heavy, processed) bring dullness and disease.
topics.daily_tip: Choose freshly prepared, lightly spiced, whole foods for 80% of meals. Reduce packaged/frozen/reheated food.

topics.title: Abhyanga - Daily Oil Massage
topics.description: Daily self-massage with warm oil (abhyanga) improves circulation, strengthens muscles, calms vata, promotes sleep, and nourishes skin. Sesame oil for vata, coconut for pitta, mustard for kapha.
topics.daily_tip: 10 minutes of self-massage before morning bath. Use sesame oil for vata, coconut for pitta, mustard/warm oil for kapha.

topics.title: Triphala - The Master Rejuvenator
topics.description: Triphala (Amalaki + Bibhitaki + Haritaki) is the greatest Ayurvedic formulation. It cleanses all three doshas, supports digestion, improves vision, and acts as a mild daily detoxifier.
topics.daily_tip: Take 1 tsp Triphala powder in warm water at bedtime. Start with small dose and increase gradually.

topics.title: Proper Sleep - The Second Pillar of Life
topics.description: Sleep (nidra) is one of three supporting pillars of life along with food and brahmacharya. Proper sleep restores the body, balances doshas, and sharpens the mind. Sleep before 10 PM is tama-sattvic transition.
topics.daily_tip: Sleep before 10 PM. Avoid screens 1 hour before bed. Apply brahmi or sesame oil to feet before sleeping.

dosha_impact.vata: irregular sleep worsens anxiety - keep fixed schedule

topics.title: Pathya Ahara - Wholesome Food Rules
topics.description: Charaka defines pathya (wholesome) as food and lifestyle that is beneficial to the channels of the body. Apathya (unwholesome) creates blockages. The stomach should be 1/2 food, 1/4 water, 1/4 empty.
topics.daily_tip: Eat until 3/4 full only. Leave 1/4 of stomach empty. Sit quietly for 5 min after eating - do not rush.

topics.title: Optimal Exercise - Half Capacity Rule
topics.description: Exercise to only half your capacity. Signs of proper exercise: light perspiration, deep breathing, feeling of lightness. Over-exercise depletes ojas (vital essence) and increases vata.
topics.daily_tip: 30-45 min morning walk or yoga. Stop when you start sweating lightly on forehead. Never exercise to exhaustion.

dosha_impact.pitta: swim or exercise in cool morning

topics.title: Sattva - Mental Strength and Clarity
topics.description: Mental strength (sattva) is the capacity to withstand stress without losing equilibrium. It is built through: sattvic food, proper sleep, mantra/meditation, positive company (satsang), and Vedic study.
topics.daily_tip: Begin each day with 10 min silent meditation or mantra chanting. Om Namah Shivaya or Gayatri Mantra are ideal.
